articles.title: Federal regulators might actually make rules for prediction markets
articles.summary: The CFTC is finally considering formal regulations for prediction markets after years of operating in a regulatory gray zone. Clear rules could legitimize the industry and attract institutional money, but they'll probably come with restrictions that kill the wild west vibe that made these markets fun in the first place.

articles.title: How Many Times Will Trump Visit Mar-a-Lago? Kalshi Odds
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering odds on how frequently Trump will visit his Florida resort during his upcoming presidency, turning presidential travel patterns into a betting market. The prop highlights how prediction platforms are expanding beyond traditional political outcomes to capture granular behavioral bets that could signal everything from Trump's governing style to his actual time spent in Washington.

articles.title: Miami vs. Virginia: Preview and Kalshi Markets for this ACC Tournament Semifinal
articles.summary: Miami and Virginia square off in the ACC Tournament semifinals, with prediction markets on Kalshi offering real-time odds on the matchup. The game matters because ACC Tournament results directly impact March Madness seeding, creating volatile betting opportunities as teams' championship odds shift with each win or loss.

articles.title: When will a U.S.–Iran ceasefire happen? Prediction markets point to early summer
articles.summary: Prediction markets are pricing in a U.S.–Iran ceasefire deal by early summer 2025, suggesting traders expect diplomatic momentum to accelerate in the coming months. This timeline creates a clear binary bet with defined odds, making it one of the cleaner geopolitical plays for traders who can stomach the volatility of Middle East peace negotiations.

articles.title: Utah's anti-gambling tradition meets Kalshi and Polymarket in a new legal fight
articles.summary: Utah officials are targeting prediction markets platforms Kalshi and Polymarket, likely arguing their betting products violate the state's strict anti-gambling laws. This could set a precedent for how other conservative states regulate prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to geo-block entire regions or face costly legal battles.

articles.title: Future is unpredictable for prediction markets as courts add to confusion
articles.summary: Courts are issuing conflicting rulings on prediction market regulations, creating legal uncertainty across jurisdictions. The patchwork of decisions makes it harder for platforms to operate confidently and could scare off institutional money that prediction markets desperately need to grow.

articles.title: CFTC Offers First Guidance on Manipulation in Prediction Markets
articles.summary: The CFTC just released its first official guidance on what constitutes market manipulation in prediction markets, giving platforms and traders clearer rules about crossing the line. This regulatory clarity should reduce legal uncertainty for prediction market operators and potentially open the door for more institutional participation in political betting.

articles.title: US commodity regulator kicks off rulemaking for prediction markets
articles.summary: The CFTC is starting formal rulemaking to establish regulations for prediction markets operating under US jurisdiction. This could finally provide the regulatory clarity that platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt need to expand their offerings without constant legal uncertainty.

articles.title: Jail break, election betting: These bets are illegal in Louisiana. But the state can't stop them.
articles.summary: Louisiana bettors are placing illegal wagers on everything from prison escapes to election outcomes, and state authorities lack the enforcement tools to shut them down. This enforcement gap highlights how prediction markets are thriving in legal gray areas, potentially setting precedent for other states struggling to regulate political betting and unconventional wagering.

articles.title: CME, Cboe chiefs urge crackdown on fast-growing prediction markets
articles.summary: The heads of CME and Cboe are pushing regulators to crack down on the booming prediction markets industry. Traditional derivatives exchanges clearly see platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket as a threat to their monopoly on event-based trading, so they're trying to strangle the competition in the regulatory crib.

articles.title: Watch out, prediction markets. The 'super apps' are coming.
articles.summary: Tech giants are building "super apps" that bundle multiple services, and they're eyeing prediction markets as their next feature. When WeChat or Meta decides to add betting to their billion-user platforms, standalone prediction market sites will face the same fate as independent photo apps after Instagram launched.

articles.title: Breakingviews - How insider-trading fears harm prediction markets
articles.summary: Regulators and critics worry that prediction markets create insider trading opportunities, where people with non-public information can profit unfairly from betting on outcomes they know in advance. These concerns are slowing mainstream adoption and regulatory approval of prediction markets, even though the fear is largely overblown compared to the actual benefits these markets provide for price discovery and information aggregation.
